entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia or other nitrogenous compounds

entities.name: CEREAL
entities.description: Grasses cultivated for their edible grains, which are the subject of nitrogen fixation studies

entities.name: N BALANCES
entities.description: Long-term measurements of nitrogen input and output used to estimate the amount of nitrogen fixed by crops

entities.name: 15N
entities.description: A stable isotope technique used for the direct measurement of nitrogen fixation

entities.name: ACETYLENE-REDUCTION ASSAYS
entities.description: A sensitive biochemical method used to quantify nitrogenase activity and nitrogen fixation

entities.name: LIGHT
entities.description: An environmental condition that influences the energy source and rate of nitrogen fixation in cereals

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: An environmental condition that influences the rate of nitrogen fixation

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: An environmental condition that influences the rate of nitrogen fixation

entities.name: PLANT GENOTYPE
entities.description: The genetic makeup of the plant which determines its ability to support nitrogen fixation

entities.name: PLANT AGE
entities.description: The developmental stage of the plant which affects the magnitude of nitrogen fixation

entities.name: COMBINED NITROGEN
entities.description: The availability of inorganic nitrogen in the soil, which can inhibit nitrogen fixation

entities.name: PLANT BREEDING
entities.description: The method of selecting and developing plant varieties with an improved ability to support nitrogen fixation

entities.name: BACTERIA
entities.description: Microorganisms that possess the enzyme nitrogenase and are responsible for nitrogen fixation

entities.name: NITROGENASE ACTIVITY
entities.description: The enzymatic process by which bacteria convert N2 to ammonia

entities.name: INOCULATIONS
entities.description: The application of specific bacteria to crops to enhance nitrogen fixation
